alkylation reagent: alkylation of thiol groups (IAA final concentration 80 mm, 1 h, RT, dark)
alkylation concentration: alkylation of thiol groups (IAA final concentration 80 mm, 1 h, RT, dark)
cleavage agent: protein digestion with trypsin (1:20 w/w, 37 °C, ON)
collision energy: fragmented by higher energy collisional dissociation with normalized collision energies of 30%
fractionation method: gel electrophoresis, or in-solution derivatization and LC–MS/MS
fragmentation method: fragmented by higher energy collisional dissociation with normalized collision energies of 30%
instrument: coupled to a Q Exactive Plus mass spectrometer equipped with an EASY-spray ion source
ionization type: coupled to a Q Exactive Plus mass spectrometer equipped with an EASY-spray ion source
labeling: The N-terminal amino acids of the LC fragments were identified through derivatization of free primary amines by reductive dimethylation.
reduction reagent: reduction of disulfide bonds (DTT final concentration 40 mm, 2 h, RT)
reduction concentration: reduction of disulfide bonds (DTT final concentration 40 mm, 2 h, RT)
